title: enqueue BEGIN IMMEDIATE pattern correctly prevents offline-queue flood race
description: store.rs enqueue() uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE via raw SQL on a dedicated connection rather than pool.begin() (deferred). This is correct: the COUNT + INSERT pair needs a write lock from the start to prevent two concurrent callers both reading count < 1000 and both inserting. A future contributor might simplify this to pool.begin() for uniformity — this should be resisted. The difference is material on SQLite: deferred transactions escalate to write only on first write, allowing the COUNT to race.
